problem: Let $ S $ be the set of all sides and diagonals of a regular pentagon. A pair of elements of $ S $ are selected at random without replacement. What is the probability that the two chosen segments have the same length?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe what "probability" means in the context of this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many sides and diagonals are there in a regular pentagon? Are they all of the same length?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If we select one segment at random, how many segments of the same length are left in the set?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many total segments are left in the set after the first segment is chosen? How many of them have the same length as the first segment?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the probability that the second segment has the same length as the first segment?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you calculate the probability of selecting two segments with the same length using the information we have discussed?
classified_questions.label: b
